homepage.text_excerpt: --> Chris Bloore MP | Redditch top of page It's an honour to have been elected as your Member of Parliament for Redditch and the Villages. Sign up for my newsletter to keep up with my latest work in the community and in Westminster. Pride in Place Have your say Join the campaign LATEST NEWS FROM CHRIS Securing £1.1M Investment to Transform HoW College I welcomed a £1.17 million funding boost for Heart of Worcestershire (HoW) College, with significant investment directed to the Redditch campus. The funding, part of the Government’s FE College Condition Allocation, will upgrade digital connectivity and accelerate the transition to net-zero infrastructure. I secured the investment to ensure students learn in a modern, sustainable environment equipped for future careers. This is about more than bricks and mortar - it’s about i In Redditch and the Villages Championing Unpaid Carers During Carers Careline Visit I recently visited Carers Careline to highlight the extraordinary work of unpaid carers in the community. Meeting with Chief Officer Erica Gibson Di Meo, I discussed the immense pressures carers face - from financial strain to mental health challenges. I praised the charity for providing essential advice and emotional support to those balancing care for loved ones with work and family life. Unpaid carers are the backbone of our communities. They often work tirelessly behind t In Redditch and the Villages Vowing to Fight for Future of Redditch Local History Museum I pledged urgent support for the Redditch Local History Museum after trustees received a three-month eviction notice. With the deadline looming in July, I warned that the town’s heritage could be lost without swift action. My office is working closely with Redditch Borough Council to identify vacant town-centre premises for the museum’s 7,000-strong collection. Our history matters. If we are serious about our Town of Culture 2028 bid, we must preserve and celebrate our herita In Redditch and the Villages 1 2 3 4 5 Chris Bloore MP Email: chris.bloore.mp@parliament.uk Phone: 01527 362 815 Follow Chris on Social Media: Privacy Policy > Acceptable Behaviour Policy > © 2024, The 1900 Creative LTD . pages.title: Advice Surgeries | Chris Bloore MP
headings: h3: How to Book an Appointment
pages.text_excerpt: --> Advice Surgeries | Chris Bloore MP top of page Advice surgeries As your Member of Parliament, I hold regular advice surgeries across the whole Redditch and the Villages constituency - covering every ward in Redditch, as well as the villages of Feckenham, Harvington & Norton, Inkberrow and Wychbold. ​ These sessions are an opportunity for you to raise issues directly with me and my team, whether they relate to local matters or personal concerns. I am committed to being accessible to all residents, wherever you live - whether in the town centre or in our villages. ​ How to Book an Appointment To request an appointment, please email me at chris.bloore.mp@parliament.uk with the following details: Your full name Your address Your contact details A short summary of your issue ​ My team will then review your enquiry and be in touch if an appointment is needed to progress your case. ​ For security reasons, the exact locations of surgeries are not shared publicly in advance. Once your appointment is confirmed, my team will provide the venue details. ​ Upcoming Surgeries ​ ​ ​ AM, Saturday 18th April - Town Centre (Redditch) AM, Saturday 2nd May - Greenlands (Redditch) PM, Friday 15th May - Inkberrow PM, Friday 29th May - Church Hill (Redditch) PM, Friday 12th June - Batchley (Redditch) ​ ​ ​ Every surgery is an opportunity to ensure your concerns are heard. If you need support or guidance, please don’t hesitate to get in touch. If we are serious about our Town of Culture 2028 bid, we must preserve and celebrate our herita In Redditch and the Villages Chris Bloore MP Apr 17 Praising Ribbons Cancer Support Group as a Lifeline I hailed Ribbons – Cancer for All as a lifeline for the community. During a visit to the group’s new venue, I met founder Kerry Hillier, who created the initiative after experiencing a lack of inclusive support during her own cancer journey. Ribbons offers a welcoming, barrier-free space for anyone affected by cancer to connect over tea and cake. Cancer can be an incredibly lonely journey. Groups like Ribbons change that - they are more than just meet-ups. pages.title: About Chris | Chris Bloore MP
headings: h2: NEED HELP WITH A LOCAL ISSUE?
pages.text_excerpt: --> About Chris | Chris Bloore MP top of page Home Advice Surgeries Latest News About Chris Raise a local issue Redditch Directory Campaigns Post delivery problems Police front desk Pride in Place Mount Pleasant road safety survey SEND reforms Health & Social Care Allergy safety Past Campaigns More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. Have your say ABOUT CHRIS I have always believed that there is much more that unites us than divides us and that, despite our sometimes polarised political discourse, I remain convinced that our country's best years are ahead of us. I've lived in North Worcestershire nearly all my life. I'm an active campaigner, having previously served as a councillor and a school governor. My first job was delivering the Redditch Advertiser; I spent the money I earned in the Kingfisher Centre and learned to drive around our many roundabouts. I even had the opportunity to play on a few occasions at the Valley for and against the Reds. Before I became your MP, I was the Chief Executive of an international Trade Association, helping to safeguard jobs and industry during the COVID-19 pandemic, working alongside Ministers from all political parties. I was fortunate to get a great start in life, and as a parent, I want every child to have the same opportunities Whether it's rebuilding our public services, including our beloved NHS, making it easier to start a business, growing the economy, making our streets safe, or making life affordable again, I know it won't be easy, and tough decisions must be made. ​ As your Member of Parliament, I am here for you, regardless of how you cast your vote. My door is always open, and I am ready to assist you in any way I can. We may not always see eye to eye, but please know that you can always reach out to me for help or advice. NEED HELP WITH A LOCAL ISSUE? My team and I are here to help in any way we can. Click the button below to raise an issue with us. pages.title: Mount Pleasant road safety survey | Chris Bloore MP
pages.text_excerpt: --> Mount Pleasant road safety survey | Chris Bloore MP top of page Road safety in Mount Pleasant During a recent public meeting, residents in your area raised concerns about the road safety on Mount Pleasant and the surrounding streets. ​ I’ve heard about issues regarding dangerous pavement parking, pushing pedestrians into the road, along with speeding, causing a hazardous situation for all pedestrians and particularly putting children at risk. I want Worcestershire County Council and the police to take action to make sure you feel safe using the streets in your neighbourhood. ​ Residents have suggested different ways to address these challenges, and I want to hear from you . I’ve put together a short survey to hear about your experiences in this area, and what you think could help. ​ Tell me about your road safety concerns First name * Last name * Email * Address * Postcode * Tell me about your road safety concerns in Mount Pleasant What do you think would help solve these issues? Yes, subscribe me to your newsletter.
